key_insight: Modified SINNs combine spectral-domain learning with harmonic-analysis-inspired scaling and embeddings to improve accuracy and coefficient recovery in high-dimensional PDEs.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a scalable alternative to classical spectral solvers and PINNs for PDE workloads where dimensionality makes accuracy or memory a bottleneck.
method: Learn directly in the spectral domain to avoid spatial derivative evaluation.
Add coefficient decay scaling to better match spectral coefficient behavior.
Use basis embeddings to improve approximation of unknown spectral coefficients.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on steady and time-dependent PDEs, showing better performance than sparse-grid spectral methods in middle dimensions with incomplete spectral information and better accuracy than PINNs in high dimensions.
watch_item: Performance is supported by numerical experiments in the abstract, so practical gains may depend on PDE type, spectral representation quality, and tuning of the added scaling/embedding components.
paper_title: Spectral-Informed Neural Networks Outperform Spectral Methods in High-dimensional PDEs
